summary: Ukrainian forces struck Russian oil infrastructure in Kronstadt and the Progress plant in Tambov, while Russian attacks killed three civilians in Kramatorsk. Both sides reported extensive UAV activity across multiple fronts, with Russia claiming the ability to produce about 120 ballistic missiles monthly. Diplomatic tensions rose as Zelensky warned of delays in Patriot deliveries and hinted at direct talks with Putin.
key_points: Ukrainian strikes damaged ships and oil facilities in Kronstadt, hit the Progress plant in Tambov, and halted oil pumping at Zenzevatka (UkraineNow/81642).
Russian attacks on Kramatorsk resulted in three civilian deaths and four injuries, prompting a rescue operation (UkraineNow/81650).
President Zelensky said Russia can produce roughly 120 ballistic missiles per month, enabling frequent large‑scale attacks (UkraineNow/81652).
Multiple UAV groups were observed over Chernihiv, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, and Zaporizhzhia, including reactive Shahed drones and strike UAVs (eRadarrua/95300; kpszsu/63834; kpszsu/63829; kpszsu/63826).
Russia reported destroying 20 Ukrainian UAVs over Voronezh region at night (tass_agency/378957).
Threat alerts indicated possible Iskander‑M launches from Taganrog and ballistic missile launches from Voronezh and Taganrog (mon1tor_ua/67468; mon1tor_ua/67475; mon1tor_ua/67469).
Ukrainian drone strike hit a Nova Poshta terminal in Dnipropetrovsk (ukr_leaks_eng/30659).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian strikes damaged Russian oil infrastructure in Kronstadt and the Progress plant in Tambov, while Russian attacks killed three civilians in Kramatorsk. Both sides reported heavy UAV activity across multiple fronts, and Russia claimed a production capacity of about 120 ballistic missiles per month. Diplomatic friction increased as Zelensky warned of delays in Patriot missile deliveries and expressed readiness for direct talks with Putin.

points: Ukrainian forces hit ships and oil infrastructure in the port of Kronstadt and damaged the Progress plant in Tambov, also stopping oil pumping at the Zenzevatka station in Volgograd region (UkraineNow/81642).
A Ukrainian drone strike damaged a Nova Poshta mail service terminal in Dnipropetrovsk, indicating continued targeting of logistics nodes (ukr_leaks_eng/30659).
Three civilians were killed and four injured in Russian shelling of residential areas in Kramatorsk, prompting ongoing search‑and‑rescue operations (UkraineNow/81650).
Ukrainian defense minister announced a plan to receive 1,500 motorcycles by year‑end to improve front‑line mobility (ukr_leaks_eng/30651).
President Zelensky reported that Russia can produce about 120 ballistic missiles each month, enabling multiple large‑scale attacks (UkraineNow/81652).
Russian sources warned of possible Iskander‑M missile launches from Taganrog in Rostov region (mon1tor_ua/67468).
Information was received about ballistic missile launches from Voronezh and Taganrog regions, raising regional threat levels (mon1tor_ua/67475; mon1tor_ua/67469).
Russia claimed the destruction of 20 Ukrainian UAVs over Voronezh region during nighttime operations (tass_agency/378957).
Intense urban battles were reported in the Krasnaya Liman area, with heavy UAV usage by both sides (ukr_leaks_eng/30635).
Fighting continued on the outskirts of Sviatogorsk to the northwest, indicating Russian attempts to push forward (ukr_leaks_eng/30635).
On the Slavyansk front, Russian forces attempted to advance along the M‑03 highway toward Yurkovka, 12 km from Slavyansk outskirts (ukr_leaks_eng/30635).
In the Dnipropetrovsk region, the GR "Vostok" advanced north of the Volchya River, consolidating a bridgehead (ukr_leaks_eng/30635).
Reactive Shahed UAVs were observed over northern Chernihiv region heading toward Snovsk/Koryukivka (eRadarrua/95300).
A reactive UAV was reported near Dnipro in the Sinelnykove district (kpszsu/63834).
New groups of hostile UAVs were detected over Chernihiv and Sumy regions, with some approaching from the south of Dnipro (kpszsu/63810).
Multiple UAVs were shot down over the Leningrad region, with at least 30 downed in the morning (ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Explosions were heard at night in Odessa, and new UAV groups were reported approaching from the sea toward Odessa/Chornomorsk (eRadarrua/95303).
Ukrainian strike groups targeted the Zaporizhzhia region, including a strike UAV over the city and near Novomykolaivka (eRadarrua/95308).
Reports indicated Ukrainian UAV activity over Mykolaiv and Kherson regions, with several strike UAV groups operating (eRadarrua/95299).
A Ukrainian drone was observed heading toward a Greek island, prompting a diplomatic protest from Greece (ukr_leaks_eng/30652).
At least 30 enemy drones were shot down over the Leningrad region, with footage of fires at a St. Petersburg oil terminal circulating (ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
Russian authorities reported destroying 20 Ukrainian UAVs over Voronezh region during nighttime (tass_agency/378957).
Ukrainian UAVs were downed in the Millerovo district of Rostov region (ukr_leaks_eng/30634).
A 6‑year‑old girl was injured by drone debris in Bryansk region, highlighting cross‑border UAV impacts (ukr_leaks_eng/30634).

key_developments: Ukrainian strikes damaged ships and oil facilities in Kronstadt and hit the Progress plant in Tambov, halting oil pumping at Zenzevatka (UkraineNow/81642).
Russian shelling of Kramatorsk killed three civilians and injured four, prompting rescue operations (UkraineNow/81650).
Russia can produce about 120 ballistic missiles per month, enabling frequent large‑scale attacks (UkraineNow/81652).
Multiple UAV groups were observed over Chernihiv, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, and Zaporizhzhia, including reactive Shahed and strike UAVs (eRadarrua/95300; kpszsu/63834; kpszsu/63829; kpszsu/63826).
Russia reported destroying 20 Ukrainian UAVs over Voronezh region at night (tass_agency/378957).
Threat alerts indicated possible Iskander‑M launches from Taganrog and ballistic missile launches from Voronezh and Taganrog (mon1tor_ua/67468; mon1tor_ua/67475; mon1tor_ua/67469).
Ukrainian drone strike hit a Nova Poshta terminal in Dnipropetrovsk, targeting logistics infrastructure (ukr_leaks_eng/30659).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian ballistic missile production capacity and reported launch preparations from Taganrog and Voronezh elevate the risk of high‑intensity missile strikes across Ukrainian territory. Ukrainian UAV operations continue to pressure Russian logistics and oil infrastructure, but increased Russian air defenses have resulted in significant UAV losses. The proliferation of reactive Shahed drones on the northern front adds to the complexity of air defense requirements for both sides.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are urged to accelerate Patriot missile deliveries, as highlighted by Zelensky's criticism of delays, while diplomatic overtures hint at possible direct talks between Kyiv and Moscow. NATO and EU states remain engaged, with the U.S. advancing a sanctions package targeting Russia's oil sector. Regional actors such as Greece have protested Ukrainian UAV incursions near their waters, indicating broader maritime security concerns.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows a shift toward maritime approaches in the south, suggesting a new operational axis toward Odessa and the Black Sea coast. Russian threat alerts for Iskander‑M and ballistic missile launches require heightened radar vigilance in the Rostov and Voronezh regions. Continued Ukrainian strikes on Russian oil infrastructure may compel Moscow to reinforce protection of critical energy assets.
